definition: reproductive fitness R_i = number of distinct proteges who served under head coach i and later became NFL head coaches (first HC season after first shared season).edges from relationships.csv parent_role=Head Coach.
censoring_caveat: mentors active recently are right-censored: their proteges may not yet have become head coaches, so their R is a lower bound. The tenure control and career-mean (vs total) WAR partly absorb this.

winning_begets_offspring.note: career-mean WAR is quality (tenure-adjusted); total WAR and tenure both grow mechanically with seasons coached, so the partial correlation of R on mean WAR net of tenure isolates whether winning per se -- not merely lasting -- begets offspring.

reproductive_rate.definition: HC offspring per head-coaching season; exposure = number of NFL head-coaching seasons (the coordinator-slot exposure that generates future-HC proteges).
reproductive_rate.note: exposure-normalized reproductive rate; the quality test is net of exposure (partial correlation controlling HC seasons).
